text: Machine learning is a way for computers to learn from data without being explicitly programmed for every task. In supervised learning, the computer is given training data, which includes input examples and their correct outputs. Using this information, it builds a model—a mathematical pattern that connects inputs to outputs. Once the model is ready, it can make predictions on new, unseen data. For example, after studying many pictures of cats and dogs, the model can predict whether a new picture shows a cat or a dog. The accuracy of these predictions is then measured to see how well the model performs.
